title: Design reconnect and resume
description: Add sequence ids, client cursors, and server replay so a dropped connection recovers without lost or duplicate state.
body: Steps: 1. Assign each message on a channel a monotonic id/sequence. 2. Have the client persist the last-applied id. 3. On reconnect the client sends last-seen id; the server replays buffered messages after it, or sends a fresh snapshot plus a new cursor if the gap is too large. 4. Make client apply idempotent so a replayed message is harmless. 5. Add jittered exponential reconnect backoff. Rollback point: fall back to full-snapshot-on-reconnect if replay is unavailable. Validation: a mid-stream kill test recovers every missed message exactly once after reconnect.
